puzzle0.title: RELAY ACCESS
puzzle0.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization, not the deprecated fragment.

puzzle1.title: RELAY POWER ROUTING
puzzle1.description: Legacy matching puzzle preserved for compatibility. V5 replaces this with a two-choice relay power decision.

event5.text: If someone is there, answer me.

event17.text: My name is Ethan Hale. I'm on ECHO Station.

event19.text: I'm wedged in a service alcove beside a relay corridor. Emergency strip on the floor. No ceiling lights.

event21.text: Something behind the wall keeps ticking every few seconds.

event24.text: It did not do that before.

event3.text: I used to keep the relay spine, power buses, and life-support routing from killing each other.

event5.text: It was a boring job when the station behaved.

event3.text: Annoying, but fair.

event5.text: I'm sending suit telemetry. If this is fake, it is a very committed fake with bad oxygen numbers.

event7.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
event7.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
event7.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

event11.text: The suit says battery is forty. I don't trust it enough to feel comforted.

event3.text: Lights went first. Then internal comms. Then the relay started throwing old voices back at me.

event5.text: Recordings. Loops. Distress calls that ended months ago.

event7.text: You are the first response that feels like a person.

choice1.text: TELL ME WHAT YOU CAN SEE.

event4.text: I needed that more than I wanted to.

event6.text: But don't trust me blindly. I'm tired, low on air, and there are gaps in my memory.

event8.text: If I sound too sure about something, make me prove it.

event1.text: Narrow corridor. Cable trays on the left wall. Some of them melted through their brackets.

event3.text: Floor is covered in gray dust. My boots leave clean marks in it.

event1.text: Relay room is past the dark stretch of corridor.

event3.text: The door needs my maintenance authorization. I can still pull one damaged crew record from the cache.

event4.title: CREW LOG: ETHAN HALE
event4.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE — CHIEF ENGINEER
RELAY MAINTENANCE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
STATUS: ACTIVE

NOTE: Secondary maintenance index lists deprecated fragment: _319.
Current authorization must be reconstructed from active record.
event4.caption: Recovered identity and maintenance fragment. Authorization appears partially damaged.

event6.text: The active line is damaged. The old fragment is still visible.

event8.text: Don't copy the old fragment whole. It is marked deprecated for a reason.

event10.text: I'm going to the relay door now.

event12.text: I need both hands free for the corridor. If I keep talking, I'll stop listening.

event3.text: Took longer than it should have. Half the corridor is blocked by fallen insulation panels.

event5.text: The ticking stopped when I passed the third service hatch.

event8.text: I don't know if that is better.

event10.text: Access panel is alive. Keypad cracked, but the first four digits still respond.

event3.text: Stop. Don't try another random number.

event5.text: The panel noticed the failed attempt. I can hear the lock cycling inside the frame.

event7.text: Look at the record again. Active authorization: seventy-three, blank, one. Deprecated fragment: three-one-nine.

event9.text: Only one digit fits the damaged spot.

choice1.text: SLOW DOWN. WALK ME THROUGH IT.

event3.text: The current code is not hidden like a riddle. It is damaged like a bad transmission.

event7.text: The old record shows three-one-nine. The system says not to use the whole old record.

event10.text: So we borrow the part that fits.

choice1.text: ASK HALE TO GUESS IT HIMSELF.

event3.text: I could also put my hand in a live junction box and hope it appreciates confidence.

event5.text: Use the record. My memory is not better than the evidence right now.

event3.text: Door opened.

event5.text: The room is colder than the corridor.

event7.text: Three relay columns. One dark. One humming low. One pulsing like a heartbeat with bad timing.

event9.text: I need to stabilize the link from inside.

event11.text: I can keep reserve power on life support and push the relay through a dirty line.

event13.text: Or I can clean up our signal by borrowing from reserve power.

event16.text: One keeps me safer. One keeps you closer.

choice0.text: KEEP RESERVE POWER ON LIFE SUPPORT.

event1.text: Good. Reserve stays with life support.

event3.text: I am annoyingly attached to breathing.

event5.text: I'll force the relay through the dirtier line. It will sound ugly, but ugly is acceptable.

event3.text: That gives us a cleaner carrier. It also makes my oxygen margin worse.

event5.text: I understand why you chose it. I just want the cost said out loud.

event3.text: I'm back.

event5.text: The relay is holding. Rough, but holding.

event7.text: Floor vibrates when the dirty line surges. Feels like standing on a sleeping animal.

event9.text: Life support stayed clean. My suit alarm finally stopped scolding me.

event11.text: There is a security cache behind the second relay column.

event16.text: I don't remember why I suddenly hate looking at it.

event3.text: I'm back.

event5.text: Your signal is clearer now. I can actually hear the silence between words.

event7.text: My suit alarm hated the power dip. It made a sound I would describe as personally disappointed.

event9.text: There is a security cache behind the second relay column.

event14.text: That memory comes with a bad feeling.

event1.text: The cache panel is under a burned insulation flap.

event3.text: I need to step away from the console and open it manually.

event5.text: If I leave the relay console alone, the carrier may drift.

event7.text: If I ignore the cache, we lose the first real clue we've found.

event3.text: I'm stepping away from the relay console. If the line warbles, don't panic.

event4.text: I was hoping you missed that.

event6.text: Something is sitting on the edge of the carrier.

event10.text: Like a person standing behind you and not breathing.

event14.text: There. It heard us notice it.

event16.text: I'm opening the cache now. Before it decides what we get to know.

event3.text: Cache was stuck. I had to pry the panel with a broken bracket.

event5.text: There is a sealed evidence packet inside.

event10.text: My hand went cold when I read it.

event3.text: The cache panel was warm. It should not be warm.

event5.text: Something scratched the inside of the insulation flap.

event7.text: I don't know how something scratches from inside a wall panel.

event1.title: SECURITY LOG
event1.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
event1.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

event3.title: MEDICAL REPORT
event3.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
event3.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

event4.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
event4.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
event4.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
event4.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choice1.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

event1.text: I can't explain something I don't remember doing.

event3.text: I know how that sounds.

event5.text: I know exactly how weak it is.

event7.text: But if I invent a cleaner answer, we both get a nicer lie and a worse chance of surviving.

event3.text: I remember red light over a door.

event5.text: I remember someone shouting my name from behind me.

event10.text: My hand was near the override handle.

backgroundEvent0.text: ASK HIM WHAT HAPPENED AFTER THE DOOR OPENED

event5.text: The channel pulled away from me.

event9.text: Something pressed into the carrier while we were reading the files.

event11.text: I need to seal the evidence packet before the cache corrupts it.

event4.text: Security log. Medical report. Camera analysis.

event9.text: Operator, if Door 12 comes up again, don't let me step around it.

event11.text: I don't trust my memory enough to protect either of us from it.

choice1.text: TELL HALE TO HOLD POSITION FIRST.

event8.text: Curiosity feels like courage until something answers back.

event12.text: We're leaving the relay room before it learns anything else from us.
